title: arabis collina subsp. rosea
intro: Arabis collina subsp. rosea, also known as rose rockcress, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Arabis collina subsp. rosea include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve breathing, and alleviate symptoms associated with respiratory conditions. The plant can be prepared for herbal use through infusion, decoction, or tincture, and is often used in traditional medicine to support respiratory health.
